title: Exercises: Cryostat Design
description: Practice complete cryostat system design including thermal analysis, wiring planning, and system integration

exercises.title: Complete Cryostat Design Tool
exercises.description: Build a comprehensive cryostat design tool that integrates thermal, electrical, and magnetic considerations. The tool should verify that a proposed design meets all specifications for a quantum computing system.
exercises.starterCode:

@dataclass
class CryostatDesign:
    """Complete cryostat design specification."""
    name: str
    n_qubits: int

    cooling_power: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)

    sc_shield: bool = False

    # Performance targets

    B_field_target: float = 1e-6  # T

exercises.solution:
import numpy as np
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Dict, List, Any

@dataclass
class CryostatDesign:
    """Complete cryostat design specification."""
    name: str
    n_qubits: int
    
    # Thermal specifications
    cooling_power: Dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)
    heat_loads: Dict[str, Dict[str, float]] = field(default_factory=dict)
    
    # Wiring
    wiring: Dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
    
    # Shielding
    shield_layers: int = 0
    sc_shield: bool = False
    
    # Performance targets
    T_base_target: float = 0.015  # K
    B_field_target: float = 1e-6  # T

class CryostatAnalyzer:
    """
    Analyze and verify a cryostat design for quantum computing.
    """
    
    COOLING_CAPACITY = {
        '50K': 35,
        '4K': 1.5,
        'still': 0.025,
        'CP': 0.0005,
        'MC': 15e-6
    }
    
    # Heat per line type at each stage (Watts)
    WIRING_HEAT = {
        'XY_drive': {'50K': 100e-6, '4K': 20e-6, 'still': 1e-6, 'CP': 0.1e-6, 'MC': 10e-9},
        'Z_flux': {'50K': 50e-6, '4K': 5e-6, 'still': 0.5e-6, 'CP': 50e-9, 'MC': 5e-9},
        'readout_in': {'50K': 100e-6, '4K': 15e-6, 'still': 0.5e-6, 'CP': 50e-9, 'MC': 5e-9},
        'readout_out': {'50K': 50e-6, '4K': 8e-3, 'still': 0.1e-6, 'CP': 10e-9, 'MC': 1e-9}
    }
    
    STAGES = ['50K', '4K', 'still', 'CP', 'MC']
    
    def __init__(self, design: CryostatDesign):
        self.design = design
    
    def calculate_wiring_heat(self) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """Calculate heat load from wiring at each stage."""
        heat = {stage: 0 for stage in self.STAGES}
        
        for wire_type, count in self.design.wiring.items():
            if wire_type in self.WIRING_HEAT:
                for stage in self.STAGES:
                    heat[stage] += count * self.WIRING_HEAT[wire_type].get(stage, 0)
        
        return heat
    
    def calculate_total_heat(self) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """Calculate total heat at each stage."""
        wiring_heat = self.calculate_wiring_heat()
        
        # Add other heat loads (radiation, conduction, etc.)
        other_heat = {
            '50K': 5.0,     # 5W from radiation and supports
            '4K': 0.1,      # 100 mW
            'still': 0.002, # 2 mW
            'CP': 0.00005,  # 50 uW
            'MC': 1e-6      # 1 uW baseline
        }
        
        total = {}
        for stage in self.STAGES:
            total[stage] = wiring_heat[stage] + other_heat[stage]
        
        return total
    
    def check_thermal_margins(self) -> Dict[str, Dict]:
        """Verify thermal margins at all stages."""
        total_heat = self.calculate_total_heat()
        results = {}
        
        for stage in self.STAGES:
            capacity = self.COOLING_CAPACITY[stage]
            load = total_heat[stage]
            margin = (capacity - load) / capacity * 100
            
            status = 'OK' if margin > 30 else 'WARNING' if margin > 0 else 'FAIL'
            
            results[stage] = {
                'capacity_W': capacity,
                'load_W': load,
                'margin_percent': margin,
                'status': status
            }
        
        return results
    
    def estimate_base_temperature(self) -> float:
        """Estimate achievable base temperature."""
        total_heat = self.calculate_total_heat()
        Q_mc = total_heat['MC']
        
        # Q = 84 * n3_dot * T^2, assume n3_dot = 500 umol/s
        n3_dot = 500e-6  # mol/s
        
        T_base = np.sqrt(Q_mc / (84 * n3_dot))
        return T_base
    
    def calculate_shielding_factor(self) -> float:
        """Calculate magnetic shielding attenuation."""
        # Mu-metal: ~50x per layer (simplified)
        mu_metal_factor = 50 ** self.design.shield_layers
        
        # SC shield adds ~100x if present
        sc_factor = 100 if self.design.sc_shield else 1
        
        return mu_metal_factor * sc_factor
    
    def check_magnetic_environment(self) -> Dict:
        """Verify magnetic field at sample."""
        B_earth = 50e-6  # T
        shielding = self.calculate_shielding_factor()
        B_residual = B_earth / shielding
        
        status = 'OK' if B_residual < self.design.B_field_target else 'FAIL'
        
        return {
            'B_earth': B_earth,
            'shielding_factor': shielding,
            'B_residual': B_residual,
            'B_target': self.design.B_field_target,
            'status': status
        }
    
    def total_line_count(self) -> int:
        """Get total wiring count."""
        return sum(self.design.wiring.values())
    
    def full_analysis(self) -> Dict:
        """Perform complete design analysis."""
        thermal = self.check_thermal_margins()
        T_base = self.estimate_base_temperature()
        magnetic = self.check_magnetic_environment()
        
        # Overall status
        thermal_ok = all(s['status'] != 'FAIL' for s in thermal.values())
        T_ok = T_base < self.design.T_base_target
        mag_ok = magnetic['status'] == 'OK'
        
        return {
            'thermal': thermal,
            'T_base_estimated': T_base,
            'T_base_target': self.design.T_base_target,
            'T_base_ok': T_ok,
            'magnetic': magnetic,
            'overall_status': 'PASS' if (thermal_ok and T_ok and mag_ok) else 'NEEDS WORK'
        }
    
    def print_report(self):
        """Print comprehensive design report."""
        d = self.design
        print("="*70)
        print(f"CRYOSTAT DESIGN REPORT: {d.name}")
        print("="*70)
        
        print(f"\n{'SYSTEM OVERVIEW':=^70}")
        print(f"  Qubit count: {d.n_qubits}")
        print(f"  Total wiring: {self.total_line_count()} lines")
        print(f"  Shield layers: {d.shield_layers} mu-metal + {'SC shield' if d.sc_shield else 'no SC'}")
        
        print(f"\n{'WIRING BREAKDOWN':=^70}")
        for wire_type, count in d.wiring.items():
            print(f"  {wire_type}: {count} lines")
        
        print(f"\n{'THERMAL ANALYSIS':=^70}")
        thermal = self.check_thermal_margins()
        wiring_heat = self.calculate_wiring_heat()
        
        print(f"\n  {'Stage':<8} {'Capacity':<12} {'Total Load':<12} {'Wiring':<12} {'Margin':<10} {'Status'}")
        print(f"  {'-'*64}")
        
        for stage in self.STAGES:
            t = thermal[stage]
            w = wiring_heat[stage]
            cap_str = f"{t['capacity_W']*1e3:.1f} mW" if t['capacity_W'] > 1e-3 else f"{t['capacity_W']*1e6:.1f} uW"
            load_str = f"{t['load_W']*1e3:.1f} mW" if t['load_W'] > 1e-3 else f"{t['load_W']*1e6:.1f} uW"
            wire_str = f"{w*1e3:.1f} mW" if w > 1e-3 else f"{w*1e6:.1f} uW"
            print(f"  {stage:<8} {cap_str:<12} {load_str:<12} {wire_str:<12} {t['margin_percent']:.0f}%{'':<5} [{t['status']}]")
        
        print(f"\n{'BASE TEMPERATURE':=^70}")
        T_base = self.estimate_base_temperature()
        print(f"  Target: {d.T_base_target*1000:.1f} mK")
        print(f"  Estimated: {T_base*1000:.1f} mK")
        print(f"  Status: {'OK' if T_base < d.T_base_target else 'FAIL'}")
        
        print(f"\n{'MAGNETIC SHIELDING':=^70}")
        mag = self.check_magnetic_environment()
        print(f"  Earth's field: {mag['B_earth']*1e6:.1f} uT")
        print(f"  Shielding factor: {mag['shielding_factor']:.0f}x")
        print(f"  Residual field: {mag['B_residual']*1e9:.1f} nT")
        print(f"  Target: <{mag['B_target']*1e6:.1f} uT")
        print(f"  Status: {mag['status']}")
        
        print(f"\n{'OVERALL ASSESSMENT':=^70}")
        analysis = self.full_analysis()
        print(f"  Status: {analysis['overall_status']}")
        
        if analysis['overall_status'] != 'PASS':
            print(f"\n  Issues to address:")
            for stage, t in thermal.items():
                if t['status'] == 'FAIL':
                    print(f"    - {stage} stage overloaded")
                elif t['status'] == 'WARNING':
                    print(f"    - {stage} stage has low margin")
            if not analysis['T_base_ok']:
                print(f"    - Base temperature exceeds target")
            if mag['status'] != 'OK':
                print(f"    - Magnetic shielding insufficient")
        
        print("\n" + "="*70)

# Create and analyze a 50-qubit system design
design = CryostatDesign(
    name="QC-50 Quantum Computer",
    n_qubits=50,
    wiring={
        'XY_drive': 50,
        'Z_flux': 50,
        'readout_in': 7,
        'readout_out': 7
    },
    shield_layers=2,
    sc_shield=True,
    T_base_target=0.015,
    B_field_target=1e-6
)

analyzer = CryostatAnalyzer(design)
analyzer.print_report()

# Also analyze a larger system
print("\n")
design_100 = CryostatDesign(
    name="QC-100 Quantum Computer",
    n_qubits=100,
    wiring={
        'XY_drive': 100,
        'Z_flux': 100,
        'readout_in': 13,
        'readout_out': 13
    },
    shield_layers=3,
    sc_shield=True,
    T_base_target=0.015,
    B_field_target=0.5e-6
)

analyzer_100 = CryostatAnalyzer(design_100)
analyzer_100.print_report()
hints: Calculate wiring heat by summing (count * heat_per_line) for each type

testCases.expected: Total ~114 lines, base T ~12-15 mK achievable

exercises.title: Scaling Analysis
exercises.description: Analyze how cryostat requirements scale with qubit count. Determine the maximum qubit count for different cryostat configurations and identify the limiting factors.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def analyze_scaling(max_qubits=1000):
    """
    Analyze how cryostat requirements scale with qubit count.
    
    Returns:
        Dictionary with scaling analysis results
    """
    # Cooling capacity at each stage
    capacity = {
        '50K': 35,       # W
        '4K': 1.5,       # W
        'still': 0.025,  # W
        'CP': 0.0005,    # W
        'MC': 15e-6      # W
    }
    
    # Heat per qubit (approximate, considering all line types)
    heat_per_qubit = {
        '50K': 300e-6,   # 300 uW
        '4K': 30e-6,     # 30 uW (excluding HEMT)
        'still': 2e-6,   # 2 uW
        'CP': 0.2e-6,    # 200 nW
        'MC': 20e-9      # 20 nW
    }
    
    # Fixed overhead (radiation, supports, etc.)
    overhead = {
        '50K': 5,        # W
        '4K': 0.1,       # W
        'still': 0.002,  # W
        'CP': 0.00005,   # W
        'MC': 1e-6       # W
    }
    
    # Your analysis code here
    # 1. For each qubit count, calculate heat at each stage
    # 2. Find the limiting stage
    # 3. Determine maximum qubits before any stage is overloaded
    pass

results = analyze_scaling()
# Print and plot results
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def analyze_scaling(max_qubits=1000):
    """
    Analyze how cryostat requirements scale with qubit count.
    """
    # Cooling capacity at each stage
    capacity = {
        '50K': 35,
        '4K': 1.5,
        'still': 0.025,
        'CP': 0.0005,
        'MC': 15e-6
    }
    
    # Heat per qubit
    heat_per_qubit = {
        '50K': 300e-6,
        '4K': 30e-6,
        'still': 2e-6,
        'CP': 0.2e-6,
        'MC': 20e-9
    }
    
    # Fixed overhead
    overhead = {
        '50K': 5,
        '4K': 0.1,
        'still': 0.002,
        'CP': 0.00005,
        'MC': 1e-6
    }
    
    # Special: readout HEMTs (1 per 8 qubits, 8mW each at 4K)
    hemt_power = 8e-3  # W per HEMT
    qubits_per_hemt = 8
    
    stages = ['50K', '4K', 'still', 'CP', 'MC']
    qubit_range = np.arange(1, max_qubits + 1, 10)
    
    results = {
        'qubit_range': qubit_range,
        'margins': {stage: [] for stage in stages},
        'max_qubits': {},
        'limiting_stage': None
    }
    
    for n_qubits in qubit_range:
        n_hemts = int(np.ceil(n_qubits / qubits_per_hemt))
        
        for stage in stages:
            # Calculate total heat
            heat = overhead[stage] + n_qubits * heat_per_qubit[stage]
            
            # Add HEMT contribution at 4K
            if stage == '4K':
                heat += n_hemts * hemt_power
            
            # Calculate margin
            margin = (capacity[stage] - heat) / capacity[stage] * 100
            results['margins'][stage].append(margin)
    
    # Find maximum qubits for each stage (80% capacity = 20% margin)
    for stage in stages:
        margins = np.array(results['margins'][stage])
        valid = margins > 20  # Want at least 20% margin
        if np.any(valid):
            max_idx = np.where(valid)[0][-1]
            results['max_qubits'][stage] = qubit_range[max_idx]
        else:
            results['max_qubits'][stage] = 0
    
    # Find overall limiting stage
    limiting = min(results['max_qubits'].items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
    results['limiting_stage'] = limiting[0]
    results['system_max_qubits'] = limiting[1]
    
    return results

# Run analysis
results = analyze_scaling(1000)

print("Cryostat Scaling Analysis")
print("="*60)

print("\nMaximum qubits by stage (with 20% margin):")
for stage, max_q in sorted(results['max_qubits'].items(), key=lambda x: x[1]):
    print(f"  {stage:6s}: {max_q:4d} qubits")

print(f"\nLimiting stage: {results['limiting_stage']}")
print(f"System maximum: {results['system_max_qubits']} qubits")

# Plot margins vs qubit count
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(14, 10))

# Plot 1: All stages
ax1 = axes[0, 0]
for stage in ['50K', '4K', 'still', 'CP', 'MC']:
    ax1.plot(results['qubit_range'], results['margins'][stage], label=stage, linewidth=2)
ax1.axhline(y=0, color='r', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5)
ax1.axhline(y=20, color='orange', linestyle=':', alpha=0.5, label='20% margin')
ax1.set_xlabel('Number of Qubits')
ax1.set_ylabel('Cooling Margin (%)')
ax1.set_title('Cooling Margin vs Qubit Count')
ax1.legend()
ax1.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
ax1.set_xlim(0, 1000)
ax1.set_ylim(-50, 100)

# Plot 2: Focus on critical stages
ax2 = axes[0, 1]
for stage in ['4K', 'MC']:
    ax2.plot(results['qubit_range'], results['margins'][stage], label=stage, linewidth=2)
ax2.axhline(y=0, color='r', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5, label='Overloaded')
ax2.axhline(y=20, color='orange', linestyle=':', alpha=0.5, label='20% margin')
ax2.set_xlabel('Number of Qubits')
ax2.set_ylabel('Cooling Margin (%)')
ax2.set_title('Critical Stages: 4K and MC')
ax2.legend()
ax2.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
ax2.set_xlim(0, 500)
ax2.set_ylim(-20, 100)

# Plot 3: Heat load breakdown for 100 qubits
ax3 = axes[1, 0]
n_qubits = 100
n_hemts = 13
heat_per_qubit = {'50K': 300e-6, '4K': 30e-6, 'still': 2e-6, 'CP': 0.2e-6, 'MC': 20e-9}
overhead = {'50K': 5, '4K': 0.1, 'still': 0.002, 'CP': 0.00005, 'MC': 1e-6}
capacity = {'50K': 35, '4K': 1.5, 'still': 0.025, 'CP': 0.0005, 'MC': 15e-6}

stages = ['50K', '4K', 'still', 'CP', 'MC']
x = np.arange(len(stages))
width = 0.35

qubit_heat = [n_qubits * heat_per_qubit[s] for s in stages]
other_heat = [overhead[s] for s in stages]
# Add HEMT at 4K
other_heat[1] += n_hemts * 8e-3

# Normalize to capacity for visualization
qubit_norm = [q/capacity[s]*100 for q, s in zip(qubit_heat, stages)]
other_norm = [o/capacity[s]*100 for o, s in zip(other_heat, stages)]

bars1 = ax3.bar(x - width/2, qubit_norm, width, label='Wiring', color='blue', alpha=0.7)
bars2 = ax3.bar(x + width/2, other_norm, width, label='Other (inc. HEMT)', color='orange', alpha=0.7)
ax3.axhline(y=80, color='r', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5, label='80% capacity')
ax3.set_xlabel('Temperature Stage')
ax3.set_ylabel('% of Cooling Capacity')
ax3.set_title(f'Heat Load Breakdown (100 qubits)')
ax3.set_xticks(x)
ax3.set_xticklabels(stages)
ax3.legend()
ax3.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis='y')

# Plot 4: Wiring count
ax4 = axes[1, 1]
qubit_counts = [10, 50, 100, 200, 500, 1000]
line_counts = []
for n in qubit_counts:
    n_xy = n
    n_z = n
    n_ro = int(np.ceil(n/8))
    line_counts.append(n_xy + n_z + 2*n_ro)  # 2x for in and out

ax4.bar(range(len(qubit_counts)), line_counts, color='green', alpha=0.7)
ax4.set_xticks(range(len(qubit_counts)))
ax4.set_xticklabels(qubit_counts)
ax4.set_xlabel('Number of Qubits')
ax4.set_ylabel('Total Cryogenic Lines')
ax4.set_title('Wiring Scaling')
ax4.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis='y')

# Add line count labels
for i, (q, l) in enumerate(zip(qubit_counts, line_counts)):
    ax4.annotate(f'{l}', xy=(i, l), ha='center', va='bottom')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Recommendations
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("Scaling Recommendations:")
print("="*60)
print(f"\n1. For >{results['system_max_qubits']} qubits, the {results['limiting_stage']} stage becomes limiting.")
print("   Solutions:")
if results['limiting_stage'] == '4K':
    print("   - Use higher multiplexing ratio for readout (fewer HEMTs)")
    print("   - Consider traveling-wave parametric amplifiers at MC")
    print("   - Upgrade to higher-capacity pulse tube")
if results['limiting_stage'] == 'MC':
    print("   - Increase attenuation at warmer stages")
    print("   - Use superconducting wiring below 4K")
    print("   - Increase 3He circulation rate")
print("\n2. Line density becomes a physical constraint above ~200 qubits")
print("   - Consider 3D integration or interposers")
print("   - Investigate optical interconnects for control")
hints: Heat scales linearly with qubit count for most sources
HEMT count scales as ceil(n_qubits / qubits_per_readout)
Find limiting stage by checking where margin first drops below threshold

testCases.expected: 4K or MC likely limiting around 100-200 qubits

exercises.title: Commissioning Checklist Generator
exercises.description: Create a comprehensive commissioning checklist and test procedure generator for a quantum computing cryostat. The tool should generate stage-by-stage verification procedures with pass/fail criteria.
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Dict
from enum import Enum

class TestCategory(Enum):
    VACUUM = "Vacuum Systems"
    THERMAL = "Thermal Performance"
    ELECTRICAL = "Electrical Systems"
    MAGNETIC = "Magnetic Environment"
    VIBRATION = "Vibration"
    SAFETY = "Safety Systems"

@dataclass
class TestProcedure:
    """A single test procedure with criteria."""
    name: str
    category: TestCategory
    description: str
    procedure: List[str]
    pass_criteria: str
    tools_required: List[str]
    estimated_time: str

class CommissioningChecklist:
    """
    Generate commissioning checklist for quantum computing cryostat.
    """
    
    def __init__(self, system_name: str, n_qubits: int):
        self.system_name = system_name
        self.n_qubits = n_qubits
        self.tests: List[TestProcedure] = []
    
    def add_standard_tests(self):
        """Add standard commissioning tests."""
        # Your code here - add tests for each category
        pass
    
    def add_custom_test(self, test: TestProcedure):
        """Add a custom test procedure."""
        self.tests.append(test)
    
    def generate_report(self) -> str:
        """Generate formatted commissioning report."""
        # Your code here
        pass
    
    def print_checklist(self):
        """Print the complete checklist."""
        # Your code here
        pass

# Generate checklist for 50-qubit system
checklist = CommissioningChecklist("QC-50", 50)
checklist.add_standard_tests()
checklist.print_checklist()
exercises.solution:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Dict
from enum import Enum

class TestCategory(Enum):
    VACUUM = "Vacuum Systems"
    THERMAL = "Thermal Performance"
    ELECTRICAL = "Electrical Systems"
    MAGNETIC = "Magnetic Environment"
    VIBRATION = "Vibration"
    SAFETY = "Safety Systems"

@dataclass
class TestProcedure:
    """A single test procedure with criteria."""
    name: str
    category: TestCategory
    description: str
    procedure: List[str]
    pass_criteria: str
    tools_required: List[str]
    estimated_time: str

class CommissioningChecklist:
    """
    Generate commissioning checklist for quantum computing cryostat.
    """
    
    def __init__(self, system_name: str, n_qubits: int):
        self.system_name = system_name
        self.n_qubits = n_qubits
        self.tests: List[TestProcedure] = []
    
    def add_standard_tests(self):
        """Add standard commissioning tests."""
        
        # VACUUM TESTS
        self.tests.append(TestProcedure(
            name="Room Temperature Leak Check",
            category=TestCategory.VACUUM,
            description="Verify vacuum integrity at room temperature",
            procedure=[
                "Close all valves and ensure system is isolated",
                "Pump down to <1e-3 mbar with scroll pump",
                "Engage turbo pump and wait for <1e-5 mbar",
                "Use helium leak detector to check all joints",
                "Record leak rate and locations of any leaks"
            ],
            pass_criteria="Leak rate < 1e-9 mbar*L/s",
            tools_required=["Helium leak detector", "Turbo pump station"],
            estimated_time="2-4 hours"
        ))
        
        self.tests.append(TestProcedure(
            name="Cold Leak Check",
            category=TestCategory.VACUUM,
            description="Verify vacuum integrity at cryogenic temperatures",
            procedure=[
                "Cool system to 4K",
                "Monitor vacuum gauge continuously",
                "If pressure rises, use RGA to identify leak source",
                "Check for virtual leaks from outgassing"
            ],
            pass_criteria="IVC pressure < 1e-5 mbar at 4K",
            tools_required=["Residual gas analyzer (RGA)"],
            estimated_time="During cooldown (24-48 hrs)"
        ))
        
        # THERMAL TESTS
        self.tests.append(TestProcedure(
            name="Cooldown Time Verification",
            category=TestCategory.THERMAL,
            description="Verify cooldown time meets specifications",
            procedure=[
                "Start with system at room temperature",
                "Begin cooldown, recording timestamps",
                "Log temperatures at all stages every hour",
                "Record time to reach 4K, 1K, and base temperature"
            ],
            pass_criteria="4K in <24 hours, base temp in <48 hours",
            tools_required=["Temperature logging system"],
            estimated_time="48 hours"
        ))
        
        self.tests.append(TestProcedure(
            name="Base Temperature Verification",
            category=TestCategory.THERMAL,
            description="Verify mixing chamber reaches specified base temperature",
            procedure=[
                "Allow system to stabilize for 12+ hours after cooldown",
                "Record mixing chamber temperature",
                "Verify all thermometers are reading correctly",
                "Check for thermal shorts or unexpected heat loads"
            ],
            pass_criteria="T_MC < 15 mK",
            tools_required=["Calibrated RuO2 thermometers"],
            estimated_time="12+ hours after cooldown"
        ))
        
        self.tests.append(TestProcedure(
            name="Cooling Power Measurement",
            category=TestCategory.THERMAL,
            description="Measure cooling power at multiple temperatures",
            procedure=[
                "Install calibrated heater on mixing chamber",
                "Apply known heat loads: 1, 5, 10, 20 uW",
                "Allow temperature to stabilize (30+ min each)",
                "Record temperature vs power curve",
                "Verify cooling power at 100 mK"
            ],
            pass_criteria="Cooling power > 300 uW at 100 mK",
            tools_required=["Precision DC source", "Calibrated heater"],
            estimated_time="4-6 hours"
        ))
        
        # ELECTRICAL TESTS
        self.tests.append(TestProcedure(
            name="DC Line Continuity",
            category=TestCategory.ELECTRICAL,
            description="Verify all DC lines are functional",
            procedure=[
                "At room temperature, measure resistance of each DC line",
                "Verify expected resistance based on wire type and length",
                "Check for shorts between lines",
                "Repeat at 4K for superconducting lines"
            ],
            pass_criteria=f"All {self.n_qubits} Z lines within spec, no shorts",
            tools_required=["4-wire resistance meter"],
            estimated_time="2-4 hours"
        ))
        
        self.tests.append(TestProcedure(
            name="RF Line S-Parameter Verification",
            category=TestCategory.ELECTRICAL,
            description="Verify RF line performance",
            procedure=[
                "Connect VNA to each RF line",
                "Measure S21 (transmission) from 1-10 GHz",
                "Verify attenuation matches design",
                "Check for resonances or unexpected features",
                "Measure S11 (reflection) at sample end"
            ],
            pass_criteria="Attenuation within +/-3 dB of design, S11 < -15 dB",
            tools_required=["Vector network analyzer"],
            estimated_time="4-8 hours"
        ))
        
        self.tests.append(TestProcedure(
            name="Noise Floor Measurement",
            category=TestCategory.ELECTRICAL,
            description="Verify electrical noise is below specification",
            procedure=[
                "Connect spectrum analyzer to output lines",
                "Measure noise floor with 50 Ohm termination at MC",
                "Check for spurious signals from 1 MHz to 10 GHz",
                "Verify HEMT noise temperature"
            ],
            pass_criteria="HEMT noise temp < 5K, no spurs > -80 dBm",
            tools_required=["Spectrum analyzer", "50 Ohm terminations"],
            estimated_time="2-4 hours"
        ))
        
        # MAGNETIC TESTS
        self.tests.append(TestProcedure(
            name="Magnetic Field Mapping",
            category=TestCategory.MAGNETIC,
            description="Verify magnetic field at sample location",
            procedure=[
                "Install 3-axis magnetometer at sample position",
                "Measure field with shields at room temperature",
                "Cool system and measure at 4K",
                "Measure at base temperature after SC shield engaged",
                "Map field across sample area if possible"
            ],
            pass_criteria="|B| < 1 uT at sample location",
            tools_required=["Fluxgate magnetometer or SQUID"],
            estimated_time="2 hours (at base temp)"
        ))
        
        # VIBRATION TESTS
        self.tests.append(TestProcedure(
            name="Vibration Spectrum Measurement",
            category=TestCategory.VIBRATION,
            description="Characterize vibration environment",
            procedure=[
                "Install accelerometer on sample stage (if possible) or MC plate",
                "Record vibration spectrum from 0.1-100 Hz",
                "Identify pulse tube fundamental and harmonics",
                "Compare to specification"
            ],
            pass_criteria="Displacement < 10 um at 1.4 Hz at sample",
            tools_required=["Low-frequency accelerometer", "Spectrum analyzer"],
            estimated_time="1-2 hours"
        ))
        
        # SAFETY TESTS
        self.tests.append(TestProcedure(
            name="Safety Interlock Verification",
            category=TestCategory.SAFETY,
            description="Verify all safety interlocks are functional",
            procedure=[
                "Test vacuum interlock (PT stops if vacuum lost)",
                "Test water flow interlock for compressor",
                "Test temperature interlocks",
                "Verify emergency stop functionality",
                "Document all interlock setpoints"
            ],
            pass_criteria="All interlocks trigger correctly",
            tools_required=["Safety test equipment"],
            estimated_time="2-4 hours"
        ))
    
    def add_custom_test(self, test: TestProcedure):
        """Add a custom test procedure."""
        self.tests.append(test)
    
    def generate_report(self) -> str:
        """Generate formatted commissioning report."""
        lines = []
        lines.append("="*70)
        lines.append(f"COMMISSIONING CHECKLIST: {self.system_name}")
        lines.append(f"Configuration: {self.n_qubits} qubits")
        lines.append("="*70)
        
        # Group by category
        by_category = {}
        for test in self.tests:
            cat = test.category.value
            if cat not in by_category:
                by_category[cat] = []
            by_category[cat].append(test)
        
        test_num = 1
        for category, tests in by_category.items():
            lines.append(f"\n{category.upper()}")
            lines.append("-"*70)
            
            for test in tests:
                lines.append(f"\n[{test_num}] {test.name}")
                lines.append(f"    Description: {test.description}")
                lines.append(f"    Pass Criteria: {test.pass_criteria}")
                lines.append(f"    Estimated Time: {test.estimated_time}")
                lines.append(f"    Tools: {', '.join(test.tools_required)}")
                lines.append(f"    Procedure:")
                for i, step in enumerate(test.procedure, 1):
                    lines.append(f"      {i}. {step}")
                lines.append(f"    [ ] PASS  [ ] FAIL  [ ] N/A")
                lines.append(f"    Notes: ____________________")
                test_num += 1
        
        lines.append("\n" + "="*70)
        lines.append("SIGN-OFF")
        lines.append("-"*70)
        lines.append("Commissioning Engineer: ____________  Date: ______")
        lines.append("Reviewed By: ____________  Date: ______")
        lines.append("="*70)
        
        return "\n".join(lines)
    
    def print_checklist(self):
        """Print the complete checklist."""
        print(self.generate_report())
    
    def summary(self):
        """Print summary statistics."""
        by_category = {}
        for test in self.tests:
            cat = test.category.value
            if cat not in by_category:
                by_category[cat] = 0
            by_category[cat] += 1
        
        print(f"\nCommissioning Summary for {self.system_name}:")
        print(f"  Total tests: {len(self.tests)}")
        for cat, count in by_category.items():
            print(f"  {cat}: {count}")

# Generate checklist for 50-qubit system
checklist = CommissioningChecklist("QC-50 Quantum Computer", 50)
checklist.add_standard_tests()

# Add a custom test
checklist.add_custom_test(TestProcedure(
    name="Qubit Spectroscopy Verification",
    category=TestCategory.ELECTRICAL,
    description="Verify qubit frequencies match design",
    procedure=[
        "Perform two-tone spectroscopy on all 50 qubits",
        "Record qubit frequencies and compare to target",
        "Check for frequency collisions",
        "Document any qubits outside specification"
    ],
    pass_criteria="All qubits within 50 MHz of target, no collisions",
    tools_required=["Quantum control system", "Spectrum analyzer"],
    estimated_time="4-8 hours"
))

checklist.summary()
print("\n")
checklist.print_checklist()

Include specific pass/fail criteria for each test
List required equipment for planning purposes

testCases.expected: Comprehensive checklist with vacuum, thermal, electrical, magnetic, vibration, and safety tests
